title: Art And Life
content:
When Art goes bounding, lean,
    Up hill-tops fired green
    To pluck a rose for life.

    Life like a broody hen
    Cluck-clucks him back again.

    But when Art, imbecile,
    Sits old and chill
    On sidings shaven clean,
    And counts his clustering
    Dead daisies on a string
    With witless laughter....

    Then like a new Jill
    Toiling up a hill
    Life scrambles after.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Lola Ridge, titled "Art And Life", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
